``````css
    .cal-tick { position: absolute; top: -2px; width: 2px; height: 16px; background: var(--text); opacity: .7; }
    .cal-val { flex: 0 0 150px; text-align: right; }
    .sb-exwin { font-size: 0.72rem; color: var(--text-dim); margin-top: 4px; }
    .sb-exwin .win { color: var(--green); } .sb-exwin .loss { color: var(--red); }
    /* Per-section decile breakdown */
    .rq-bysec { margin-top: 10px; border-top: 1px dashed var(--border); padding-top: 8px; }
    .rq-bysec-h { margin-bottom: 6px; }
    .rq-sec { margin: 2px 0; border: 1px solid var(--border); border-radius: 8px; background: var(--card); }
    .rq-sec > summary { cursor: pointer; list-style: none; display: grid; grid-template-columns: 16px minmax(120px, 1.6fr) auto auto auto auto; gap: 8px; align-items: baseline; padding: 6px 9px; font-size: 0.72rem; }
    .rq-sec > summary::-webkit-details-marker { display: none; }
    .rq-sec > summary::before { content: '▸'; color: var(--text-dim); }
    .rq-sec[open] > summary::before { content: '▾'; }
    .rq-sec-v { text-align: center; }
    .rq-sec-nm { font-weight: 600; color: var(--text); }
    .rq-sec-ic b { font-weight: 800; }
    .rq-sec-body { padding: 4px 9px 9px; }
    @media (max-width: 640px) { .rq-sec > summary { grid-template-columns: 16px 1fr auto; } .rq-sec-m, .rq-sec-sp { display: none; } }

    .td-short { color: var(--red) !important; font-weight: 700; }

    .td-evt-chip { font-size: 0.68rem; }
    .td-evt-chip.binary, .td-evt.binary { color: var(--amber) !important; font-weight: 700; }

/* ── Evidence & Maturity — earned trust grade per strategy ─────────────────── */
.mat-badge { display:inline-flex; align-items:center; gap:.25em; font-size:.7rem; font-weight:700; padding:.1em .5em; border-radius:999px; white-space:nowrap; border:1px solid transparent; }
.mat-validated    { background:rgba(34,197,94,.14);  color:#22c55e; border-color:rgba(34,197,94,.4); }
.mat-promising    { background:rgba(250,204,21,.14);  color:#eab308; border-color:rgba(250,204,21,.4); }
.mat-experimental { background:rgba(139,92,246,.14);  color:#a78bfa; border-color:rgba(139,92,246,.4); }
.mat-informational{ background:rgba(148,163,184,.14); color:#94a3b8; border-color:rgba(148,163,184,.4); }
.mat-disabled     { background:rgba(239,68,68,.14);   color:#ef4444; border-color:rgba(239,68,68,.4); }
.mat-legend { display:flex; flex-wrap:wrap; gap:.5rem; margin:.25rem 0 .75rem; }
.mat-leg { font-size:.72rem; font-weight:600; padding:.2em .6em; border-radius:999px; }
.mat-leg b { font-weight:800; margin-left:.2em; }
.mat-leg.mat-validated{background:rgba(34,197,94,.12);color:#22c55e;} .mat-leg.mat-promising{background:rgba(250,204,21,.12);color:#eab308;} .mat-leg.mat-experimental{background:rgba(139,92,246,.12);color:#a78bfa;} .mat-leg.mat-informational{background:rgba(148,163,184,.12);color:#94a3b8;} .mat-leg.mat-disabled{background:rgba(239,68,68,.12);color:#ef4444;}
.mat-intro { font-size:.8rem; color:var(--muted,#94a3b8); line-height:1.5; margin-bottom:1rem; }
.mat-grid { display:grid; grid-template-columns:repeat(auto-fill,minmax(260px,1fr)); gap:.6rem; }
.mat-card { background:rgba(255,255,255,.02); border:1px solid rgba(255,255,255,.08); border-radius:10px; padding:.6rem .7rem; }
.mat-card-h { display:flex; align-items:center; gap:.4rem; flex-wrap:wrap; margin-bottom:.35rem; }
.mat-card-h b { font-size:.9rem; }
.mat-core { font-size:.6rem; text-transform:uppercase; letter-spacing:.04em; background:rgba(96,165,250,.16); color:#60a5fa; padding:.05em .4em; border-radius:4px; font-weight:700; }
.mat-hz { margin-left:auto; font-size:.68rem; color:var(--muted,#94a3b8); background:rgba(255,255,255,.04); padding:.1em .45em; border-radius:4px; }
.mat-reason { font-size:.76rem; color:var(--muted,#cbd5e1); line-height:1.45; }
.mat-track { font-size:.74rem; margin-top:.35rem; color:var(--muted,#94a3b8); }
.mat-track .mat-pos { color:#22c55e; font-weight:700; } .mat-track .mat-neg { color:#ef4444; font-weight:700; }
.mat-crit { font-size:.72rem; margin-top:.35rem; color:#a78bfa; }
.mat-lab { border-color:rgba(139,92,246,.35) !important; }
.mat-lab-note { font-size:.76rem; color:var(--muted,#94a3b8); line-height:1.45; margin:.25rem 0 .6rem; }

/* Model governance — the actionable status that controls sizing weight */
.gov-panel { background:rgba(255,255,255,.02); border:1px solid rgba(255,255,255,.1); border-radius:10px; padding:.6rem .75rem; margin:0 0 1rem; }
.gov-panel-h { display:flex; align-items:center; gap:.5rem; font-weight:700; font-size:.86rem; margin-bottom:.5rem; }
.gov-legend { display:flex; flex-wrap:wrap; gap:.4rem; margin-bottom:.5rem; }
.gov-leg { font-size:.7rem; font-weight:600; padding:.18em .55em; border-radius:999px; }
.gov-leg b { font-weight:800; margin-left:.2em; }
.gov-badge { display:inline-flex; align-items:center; gap:.2em; font-size:.66rem; font-weight:700; padding:.08em .45em; border-radius:999px; white-space:nowrap; border:1px solid transparent; cursor:help; }
.gov-production,.gov-leg.gov-production { background:rgba(34,197,94,.14); color:#22c55e; }
.gov-reduced,.gov-leg.gov-reduced { background:rgba(59,130,246,.14); color:#60a5fa; }
.gov-probation,.gov-leg.gov-probation { background:rgba(249,115,22,.14); color:#fb923c; }
.gov-paper,.gov-leg.gov-paper { background:rgba(148,163,184,.14); color:#94a3b8; }
.gov-disabled,.gov-leg.gov-disabled { background:rgba(239,68,68,.14); color:#ef4444; }
.gov-retired,.gov-leg.gov-retired { background:rgba(120,113,108,.14); color:#a8a29e; }

/* Today-card evidence grade + universe-percentile chips (score-honesty pass) */
.td-grade { font-size:.62rem; font-weight:700; padding:.08em .4em; border-radius:999px; border:1px solid transparent; }
.td-grade.mat-validated{background:rgba(34,197,94,.14);color:#22c55e;border-color:rgba(34,197,94,.4);} .td-grade.mat-promising{background:rgba(250,204,21,.14);color:#eab308;border-color:rgba(250,204,21,.4);} .td-grade.mat-experimental{background:rgba(139,92,246,.14);color:#a78bfa;border-color:rgba(139,92,246,.4);} .td-grade.mat-informational{background:rgba(148,163,184,.14);color:#94a3b8;} .td-grade.mat-disabled{background:rgba(239,68,68,.14);color:#ef4444;border-color:rgba(239,68,68,.4);}
.td-pctile { font-size:.62rem; font-weight:700; color:#60a5fa; background:rgba(96,165,250,.12); padding:.08em .4em; border-radius:999px; }

/* Data-trust provenance panel on the Today command center */
.dt-trust { margin:.6rem 0; border:1px solid rgba(255,255,255,.08); border-radius:10px; background:rgba(255,255,255,.02); }
.dt-trust summary { cursor:pointer; padding:.55rem .75rem; font-size:.78rem; font-weight:600; color:var(--muted,#cbd5e1); }
.dt-trust-body { padding:0 .75rem .7rem; }
.dt-trust-note { font-size:.75rem; color:var(--amber,#f59e0b); margin:.2rem 0 .6rem; line-height:1.45; }
.dt-src { display:grid; grid-template-columns:1.4fr 1.6fr .9fr; gap:.5rem; align-items:center; font-size:.72rem; padding:.22rem 0; border-top:1px solid rgba(255,255,255,.05); }
.dt-src:first-child { border-top:none; }
.dt-src-st { text-align:right; color:#22c55e; } .dt-src-st.stale { color:var(--amber,#f59e0b); }
.dt-leg-h { font-size:.72rem; font-weight:700; margin:.7rem 0 .3rem; color:var(--muted,#cbd5e1); }
.dt-leg { font-size:.72rem; padding:.18rem 0; line-height:1.4; }
@media (max-width:640px){ .dt-src { grid-template-columns:1fr; gap:.1rem; } .dt-src-st { text-align:left; } }

/* Baseline validation tab */
.bl-list, .bl-slist { display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:.1rem; }
.bl-row { display:grid; grid-template-columns:1.3fr 1.4fr 2fr; gap:.6rem; align-items:baseline; font-size:.76rem; padding:.32rem 0; border-top:1px solid rgba(255,255,255,.05); }
.bl-row:first-child { border-top:none; }
.bl-name { font-weight:700; }
.bl-edge { font-weight:600; } .bl-edge.bl-pos { color:#22c55e; } .bl-edge.bl-dead { color:#94a3b8; }
.bl-pos { color:#22c55e; font-weight:700; } .bl-neg { color:#ef4444; font-weight:700; }
.bl-na { opacity:.7; }
.bl-srow { display:grid; grid-template-columns:1.3fr auto 1fr 1fr auto auto; gap:.55rem; align-items:center; font-size:.76rem; padding:.32rem 0; border-top:1px solid rgba(255,255,255,.05); }
.bl-cell { color:var(--muted,#cbd5e1); } .bl-clear { font-size:.95rem; }
.bl-actions { margin:.7rem 0; }
@media (max-width:640px){ .bl-row, .bl-srow { grid-template-columns:1fr; gap:.1rem; } }

/* ── EVOLVE — Adaptive Pre-Move Discovery Engine ─────────────────────────── */
.ev-wrap { display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:.85rem; }
.ev-intro { background:var(--surface-2,#161b26); border:1px solid var(--border,#2a3240); border-left:3px solid #8b5cf6; border-radius:8px; padding:.75rem .9rem; font-size:.9rem; line-height:1.5; color:var(--text-dim,#b8c2d0); }
.ev-intro b { color:var(--text,#e8edf4); }
.ev-regime { display:flex; flex-wrap:wrap; align-items:center; gap:.6rem; padding:.5rem .2rem; }
.ev-reg-label { font-size:1rem; } .ev-reg-label b { color:#8b5cf6; }
.ev-reg-sup { font-size:.8rem; color:var(--text-muted,#8590a0); }
.ev-dims { display:flex; flex-wrap:wrap; gap:.3rem; }
.ev-dim { font-size:.68rem; font-family:var(--font-mono,monospace); background:var(--card,#1a2030); border:1px solid var(--border,#2a3240); border-radius:5px; padding:.1rem .35rem; color:var(--text-dim,#b8c2d0); }
.ev-dim-un { color:var(--text-muted,#8590a0); font-style:italic; }
.ev-health { display:flex; flex-wrap:wrap; gap:.6rem; font-size:.8rem; color:var(--text-dim,#b8c2d0); padding:.1rem; }
.ev-h-item b { color:var(--text,#e8edf4); }
.ev-h-warn { color:var(--amber,#f59e0b); }
.ev-empty { text-align:center; padding:2rem 1rem; background:var(--surface-2,#161b26); border:1px dashed var(--border,#2a3240); border-radius:10px; }
.ev-empty-icon { font-size:2rem; } .ev-empty b { display:block; margin:.4rem 0; color:var(--text,#e8edf4); }
.ev-empty p { font-size:.88rem; color:var(--text-dim,#b8c2d0); max-width:640px; margin:0 auto; line-height:1.5; }
.ev-col { margin-top:.4rem; }
.ev-col-head { font-weight:600; color:var(--text,#e8edf4); border-bottom:1px solid var(--border,#2a3240); padding-bottom:.3rem; margin-bottom:.5rem; }
.ev-col-bar { font-size:.72rem; font-weight:400; color:var(--text-muted,#8590a0); font-family:var(--font-mono,monospace); margin-left:.4rem; }
.ev-col-empty { font-size:.82rem; color:var(--text-muted,#8590a0); padding:.3rem 0 .6rem; }
.ev-card { background:var(--card,#1a2030); border:1px solid var(--border,#2a3240); border-left:3px solid var(--border,#2a3240); border-radius:8px; margin-bottom:.5rem; overflow:hidden; }
.ev-card.ev-trade { border-left-color:var(--green,#22c55e); }
.ev-card.ev-probe { border-left-color:var(--cyan,#22d3ee); }
.ev-card.ev-watch { border-left-color:var(--amber,#f59e0b); }
.ev-card.ev-abstain { border-left-color:var(--text-muted,#8590a0); opacity:.85; }
.ev-card-head { display:flex; align-items:center; gap:.6rem; padding:.5rem .7rem; cursor:pointer; }
.ev-tk { font-weight:700; font-size:1rem; color:var(--accent,#60a5fa); cursor:pointer; }
.ev-tk:hover { text-decoration:underline; }
.ev-dec { font-size:.78rem; color:var(--text-dim,#b8c2d0); }
.ev-prob { margin-left:auto; font-weight:700; font-size:1.05rem; color:var(--text,#e8edf4); font-family:var(--font-mono,monospace); }
.ev-toggle { color:var(--text-muted,#8590a0); font-size:.8rem; transition:transform .15s; }
.ev-card.ev-open .ev-toggle { transform:rotate(180deg); }
.ev-card-sub { display:flex; flex-wrap:wrap; gap:.5rem; padding:0 .7rem .5rem; font-size:.76rem; color:var(--text-dim,#b8c2d0); }
.ev-spec { font-size:.9rem; margin-right:.1rem; }
.ev-payoff { font-family:var(--font-mono,monospace); }
.ev-liq { color:var(--amber,#f59e0b); }
.ev-card-body { display:none; padding:.1rem .7rem .7rem; border-top:1px solid var(--border,#2a3240); font-size:.84rem; line-height:1.5; color:var(--text-dim,#b8c2d0); }
.ev-card.ev-open .ev-card-body { display:block; }
.ev-card-body b { color:var(--text,#e8edf4); }
.ev-why { margin:.5rem 0; }
.ev-reasons ul { margin:.2rem 0 .2rem 1.1rem; padding:0; }
.ev-risk { color:var(--amber,#f59e0b); margin:.4rem 0; }
.ev-plan { font-family:var(--font-mono,monospace); font-size:.8rem; margin:.4rem 0; }
.ev-meta-row { display:flex; flex-wrap:wrap; gap:.6rem; align-items:center; margin-top:.5rem; font-size:.74rem; color:var(--text-muted,#8590a0); }
.ev-open-tk { margin-left:auto; background:none; border:1px solid var(--border,#2a3240); color:var(--accent,#60a5fa); border-radius:5px; padding:.15rem .5rem; cursor:pointer; font-size:.74rem; }
.ev-open-tk:hover { background:var(--card-hover,#222a3a); }
.ev-abstained, .ev-disclosure { background:var(--surface-2,#161b26); border:1px solid var(--border,#2a3240); border-radius:8px; padding:.5rem .8rem; font-size:.85rem; }
.ev-abstained summary, .ev-disclosure summary { cursor:pointer; color:var(--text-dim,#b8c2d0); font-weight:600; }
.ev-abstained ul { margin:.5rem 0 .2rem 1.1rem; color:var(--text-dim,#b8c2d0); }
.ev-disc-body { margin-top:.5rem; color:var(--text-dim,#b8c2d0); line-height:1.55; }
.ev-disc-body b { color:var(--text,#e8edf4); } .ev-disc-body p { margin:.5rem 0; }
.ev-dim-note { font-size:.78rem; color:var(--text-muted,#8590a0); margin-top:.3rem; }

/* ── OMEGA Evidence panel (§20 champion-vs-challenger walk-forward) ───────── */
.evi-panel summary { color:#8b5cf6; }
.evi-warn { font-size:.8rem; color:var(--amber,#f59e0b); margin:.4rem 0; }
.evi-run-btn { border-color:#8b5cf6; color:#8b5cf6; }
.evi-run-btn:hover { background:rgba(139,92,246,.12); }
.evi-run-btn:disabled { opacity:.6; cursor:progress; }
.evi-controls { display:flex; align-items:center; gap:.6rem; flex-wrap:wrap; margin:.5rem 0; }
.evi-range-label { font-size:.74rem; color:var(--text-muted,#8590a0); text-transform:uppercase; letter-spacing:.03em; }
.evi-range { display:inline-flex; border:1px solid var(--border,#2a3240); border-radius:7px; overflow:hidden; }
.evi-range-btn { background:transparent; color:var(--text-dim,#b8c2d0); border:0; border-right:1px solid var(--border,#2a3240); padding:3px 12px; font-size:.76rem; font-weight:700; cursor:pointer; }
.evi-range-btn:last-child { border-right:0; }
.evi-range-btn:hover:not(:disabled) { background:rgba(139,92,246,.1); }
.evi-range-btn.evi-range-on { background:#8b5cf6; color:#fff; }
.evi-range-btn:disabled { opacity:.5; cursor:not-allowed; }
.evi-wrap { margin-top:.7rem; display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:.7rem; }
.evi-verdict { border-radius:8px; padding:.6rem .8rem; border:1px solid var(--border,#2a3240); border-left-width:3px; font-size:.86rem; line-height:1.5; color:var(--text-dim,#b8c2d0); }
.evi-verdict-head { font-size:.95rem; color:var(--text,#e8edf4); margin-bottom:.2rem; }
.evi-verdict p { margin:0; }
.evi-pass { border-left-color:#22c55e; } .evi-fail { border-left-color:#ef4444; }
.evi-warn { border-left-color:#f59e0b; } .evi-muted { border-left-color:#6b7280; }
.evi-section { background:var(--surface-2,#161b26); border:1px solid var(--border,#2a3240); border-radius:8px; padding:.6rem .8rem; }
.evi-sec-title { font-size:.82rem; font-weight:700; color:var(--text,#e8edf4); margin-bottom:.45rem; text-transform:uppercase; letter-spacing:.03em; }
.evi-cc { display:grid; grid-template-columns:repeat(auto-fit,minmax(150px,1fr)); gap:.5rem; }
.evi-cc-cell { display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:.15rem; background:var(--surface,#0f1320); border-radius:6px; padding:.4rem .55rem; }
.evi-k { font-size:.72rem; color:var(--text-muted,#8590a0); }
.evi-v { font-size:1rem; font-weight:700; color:var(--text,#e8edf4); font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums; }
.evi-v-wide { font-size:.8rem; font-weight:600; }
.evi-muted-v { color:var(--text-muted,#8590a0); }
.evi-note { font-size:.8rem; color:var(--text-dim,#b8c2d0); line-height:1.5; margin:.5rem 0 0; }
.evi-pass-text { color:#22c55e; } .evi-fail-text { color:#f87171; }
.evi-badge { font-size:.66rem; font-weight:800; padding:2px 7px; border-radius:10px; margin-left:.4rem; vertical-align:middle; letter-spacing:.04em; }
.evi-badge-pass { background:rgba(34,197,94,.15); color:#4ade80; }
.evi-badge-hold { background:rgba(107,114,128,.18); color:#9ca3af; }
.evi-table-scroll { overflow-x:auto; }
.evi-table { width:100%; border-collapse:collapse; font-size:.8rem; }
.evi-table th { text-align:left; color:var(--text-muted,#8590a0); font-weight:600; padding:.25rem .5rem; border-bottom:1px solid var(--border,#2a3240); }
.evi-table td { padding:.3rem .5rem; border-bottom:1px solid rgba(42,50,64,.4); color:var(--text-dim,#b8c2d0); font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums; }
.evi-pos { color:#4ade80; } .evi-neg { color:#f87171; }
.evi-hv { font-size:.7rem; font-weight:700; padding:1px 6px; border-radius:8px; }
.evi-hv-pass { background:rgba(34,197,94,.15); color:#4ade80; }
.evi-hv-fail { background:rgba(239,68,68,.15); color:#f87171; }
.evi-hv-warn { background:rgba(245,158,11,.15); color:#fbbf24; }
.evi-hv-muted { background:rgba(107,114,128,.15); color:#9ca3af; }
.evi-inline { display:grid; grid-template-columns:repeat(auto-fit,minmax(220px,1fr)); gap:.9rem; }
.evi-inline > div .evi-note { margin-top:.3rem; }
.evi-promotion { border-left:3px solid #8b5cf6; }
.evi-foot { font-size:.72rem; color:var(--text-muted,#8590a0); text-align:center; padding-top:.2rem; }

/* ── Momentum Ignition ───────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
.ig-banner { background:var(--surface-2,#161b26); border:1px solid var(--border,#2a3240); border-left:3px solid #f97316; border-radius:8px; padding:.7rem .9rem; font-size:.9rem; line-height:1.5; color:var(--text-dim,#b8c2d0); margin-bottom:.7rem; }
.ig-banner b { color:var(--text,#e8edf4); }
.ig-note { margin-top:.4rem; font-size:.8rem; color:var(--amber,#f59e0b); }
.ig-controls { display:flex; gap:1rem; flex-wrap:wrap; margin-bottom:.5rem; font-size:.85rem; color:var(--text-dim,#b8c2d0); }
.ig-controls select { background:var(--card,#1a2030); color:var(--text,#e8edf4); border:1px solid var(--border,#2a3240); border-radius:5px; padding:.15rem .4rem; }
.ig-table-wrap { overflow-x:auto; border:1px solid var(--border,#2a3240); border-radius:8px; }
.ig-table { width:100%; border-collapse:collapse; font-size:.82rem; min-width:820px; }
.ig-table th { text-align:right; padding:.45rem .5rem; color:var(--text-muted,#8590a0); font-weight:600; border-bottom:1px solid var(--border,#2a3240); white-space:nowrap; position:sticky; top:0; background:var(--surface-2,#161b26); }
.ig-table th:first-child, .ig-table td:first-child { text-align:left; }
.ig-table td { text-align:right; padding:.4rem .5rem; border-bottom:1px solid var(--border,#222a3a); white-space:nowrap; }
.ig-table tbody tr { cursor:pointer; }
.ig-table tbody tr:hover { background:var(--card-hover,#222a3a); }
.ig-tk b { color:var(--accent,#60a5fa); }
.ig-score { font-weight:700; color:var(--text,#e8edf4); font-family:var(--font-mono,monospace); }
.ig-pos { color:var(--green,#22c55e); }
.ig-neg { color:var(--red,#ef4444); }
.ig-dim { color:var(--text-muted,#8590a0); }
.ig-na { color:var(--text-muted,#8590a0); font-style:italic; font-size:.75rem; }
.ig-watch { color:#60a5fa; } .ig-ign { color:#f97316; font-weight:600; } .ig-press { color:#22c55e; font-weight:600; } .ig-ext { color:var(--amber,#f59e0b); }
.ig-cat { color:var(--cyan,#22d3ee); } .ig-cat.ig-fresh { font-weight:700; }
.ig-nocat { color:var(--text-muted,#8590a0); }
.ig-empty { text-align:center; padding:1.5rem; color:var(--text-muted,#8590a0); background:var(--surface-2,#161b26); border:1px dashed var(--border,#2a3240); border-radius:8px; }
.ig-consol { margin-top:.8rem; background:var(--surface-2,#161b26); border:1px solid var(--border,#2a3240); border-radius:8px; padding:.5rem .8rem; font-size:.85rem; }
.ig-consol summary { cursor:pointer; color:var(--text-dim,#b8c2d0); font-weight:600; }
.ig-consol-body { margin-top:.5rem; color:var(--text-dim,#b8c2d0); line-height:1.55; }
.ig-consol-body b { color:var(--text,#e8edf4); } .ig-consol-body ul { margin:.3rem 0 .3rem 1.1rem; } .ig-consol-body li { margin:.25rem 0; }

/* ── OMEGA-SWING ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
.om-banner { background:var(--surface-2,#161b26); border:1px solid var(--border,#2a3240); border-left:3px solid #7c3aed; border-radius:8px; padding:.7rem .9rem; font-size:.9rem; line-height:1.5; color:var(--text-dim,#b8c2d0); margin-bottom:.8rem; }
.om-banner b { color:var(--text,#e8edf4); }
.om-note { margin-top:.4rem; font-size:.8rem; color:var(--amber,#f59e0b); }
.om-empty, .om-tier-empty { text-align:center; padding:1.2rem; color:var(--text-muted,#8590a0); background:var(--surface-2,#161b26); border:1px dashed var(--border,#2a3240); border-radius:8px; margin-bottom:.6rem; }
.om-tier-empty { padding:.7rem; font-size:.85rem; }
.om-tier-head { display:flex; align-items:baseline; gap:.6rem; flex-wrap:wrap; margin:1rem 0 .5rem; }
.om-tier-head h3 { margin:0; font-size:1rem; color:var(--text,#e8edf4); }
.om-count { font-family:var(--font-mono,monospace); color:#a78bfa; font-size:.85rem; }
.om-tier-blurb { font-size:.78rem; color:var(--text-muted,#8590a0); }
.om-cards { display:grid; grid-template-columns:repeat(auto-fill,minmax(320px,1fr)); gap:.7rem; }
.om-card { background:var(--surface,#1a2030); border:1px solid var(--border,#2a3240); border-radius:10px; padding:.7rem .8rem; font-size:.85rem; }
.om-card.om-OMEGA_PRIME { border-left:3px solid #7c3aed; }
.om-card.om-OMEGA_QUALIFIED { border-left:3px solid #22c55e; }
.om-card.om-OMEGA_WATCH { border-left:3px solid #60a5fa; }
.om-card-top { display:flex; align-items:center; gap:.5rem; }
.om-id { cursor:pointer; display:flex; flex-direction:column; flex:1; min-width:0; }
.om-tk { color:var(--accent,#a78bfa); font-size:1rem; }
.om-co { color:var(--text-muted,#8590a0); font-size:.72rem; white-space:nowrap; overflow:hidden; text-overflow:ellipsis; }
.om-px { font-family:var(--font-mono,monospace); font-size:.82rem; color:var(--text,#e8edf4); white-space:nowrap; }
.om-score { font-weight:700; font-family:var(--font-mono,monospace); font-size:1.05rem; color:#a78bfa; min-width:2.2rem; text-align:right; }
.om-pos { color:var(--green,#22c55e); } .om-neg { color:var(--red,#ef4444); }
.om-chips { display:flex; flex-wrap:wrap; gap:.3rem; margin:.5rem 0; }
.om-chip { font-size:.72rem; padding:.1rem .4rem; border-radius:4px; background:var(--surface-2,#161b26); border:1px solid var(--border,#2a3240); color:var(--text-dim,#b8c2d0); }
.om-chip.om-entry { background:rgba(124,58,237,.14); border-color:#7c3aed; color:#c4b5fd; }
.om-chip.om-setup { color:var(--cyan,#22d3ee); }
.om-stats { display:grid; grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr; gap:.15rem .8rem; margin:.5rem 0; }
.om-stat { display:flex; justify-content:space-between; gap:.4rem; font-size:.78rem; }
.om-stat-l { color:var(--text-muted,#8590a0); }
.om-stat-v { font-family:var(--font-mono,monospace); color:var(--text,#e8edf4); }
.om-plan { border-top:1px solid var(--border,#222a3a); margin-top:.4rem; padding-top:.4rem; }
.om-plan-row { display:flex; justify-content:space-between; font-size:.78rem; padding:.08rem 0; }
.om-plan-row span { color:var(--text-muted,#8590a0); } .om-plan-row b { font-family:var(--font-mono,monospace); }
.om-why { margin-top:.45rem; font-size:.78rem; color:var(--text-dim,#b8c2d0); }
.om-why b { color:var(--text,#e8edf4); }
.om-reasons { margin:.4rem 0 0 1rem; padding:0; font-size:.78rem; color:var(--text-dim,#b8c2d0); }
.om-reasons li { margin:.15rem 0; }
.om-cat { margin-top:.4rem; font-size:.78rem; color:var(--cyan,#22d3ee); }
.om-risks { margin-top:.4rem; font-size:.76rem; color:var(--amber,#f59e0b); }
.om-foot { display:flex; justify-content:space-between; margin-top:.45rem; padding-top:.35rem; border-top:1px solid var(--border,#222a3a); font-size:.72rem; color:var(--text-muted,#8590a0); }
.om-evidence { margin-top:1rem; background:var(--surface-2,#161b26); border:1px solid var(--border,#2a3240); border-radius:8px; padding:.5rem .8rem; font-size:.85rem; }
.om-evidence summary { cursor:pointer; color:var(--text-dim,#b8c2d0); font-weight:600; }
.om-evidence-body { margin-top:.5rem; color:var(--text-dim,#b8c2d0); line-height:1.55; }
.om-evidence-body b { color:var(--text,#e8edf4); } .om-evidence-body code { background:var(--card,#1a2030); padding:.05rem .3rem; border-radius:3px; font-size:.8rem; }

/* ── Measured-redundancy panel (Today) ─────────────────────────────────────── */
/* Mirrors .dt-trust: collapsed by default, informs without crowding the daily read. */
.td-redunp { margin:.6rem 0; border:1px solid rgba(255,255,255,.08); border-radius:10px; background:rgba(255,255,255,.02); }
.td-redunp summary { cursor:pointer; padding:.55rem .75rem; font-size:.78rem; font-weight:600; color:var(--muted,#cbd5e1); }
.td-redunp-body { padding:0 .75rem .7rem; }
.td-redunp-note { font-size:.75rem; color:var(--muted,#cbd5e1); margin:.2rem 0 .6rem; line-height:1.45; }
.td-redunp-warn { font-size:.75rem; color:var(--amber,#f59e0b); background:rgba(245,158,11,.07); border-left:3px solid var(--amber,#f59e0b); padding:.4rem .55rem; border-radius:6px; margin:.4rem 0 .6rem; line-height:1.45; }
.td-redunp-ok { font-size:.75rem; color:var(--green,#10d98a); margin:.3rem 0 .6rem; line-height:1.45; }
.td-redunp-grid { display:grid; grid-template-columns:repeat(auto-fit,minmax(120px,1fr)); gap:.45rem; margin:.5rem 0; }
.td-redunp-grid > div { display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:.1rem; font-size:.75rem; background:rgba(255,255,255,.03); border-radius:8px; padding:.35rem .5rem; }
.td-redunp-btn { font-size:.72rem; font-weight:600; cursor:pointer; color:var(--blue,#4d91f5); background:rgba(77,145,245,.08); border:1px solid rgba(77,145,245,.25); border-radius:7px; padding:.35rem .6rem; }
.td-redunp-btn:hover { background:rgba(77,145,245,.16); }
.td-redunp-pairs { margin:.4rem 0; overflow-x:auto; }
.td-redunp-tbl { width:100%; border-collapse:collapse; font-size:.72rem; }
.td-redunp-tbl th { text-align:left; font-weight:700; color:var(--muted,#cbd5e1); padding:.3rem .4rem; border-bottom:1px solid rgba(255,255,255,.1); white-space:nowrap; }
.td-redunp-tbl td { padding:.3rem .4rem; border-top:1px solid rgba(255,255,255,.05); }
.td-redunp-tbl tr.pr-dupe td { color:var(--amber,#f59e0b); }
.td-redunp-tbl tr.pr-indep td { color:var(--green,#10d98a); }
.td-redunp-tbl tr.pr-prior td { opacity:.6; }
.td-redunp-drift { font-size:.66rem; opacity:.75; margin-left:.3rem; white-space:nowrap; }
.td-redunp-cov, .td-redunp-foot { font-size:.68rem; margin-top:.45rem; line-height:1.4; }
/* Per-card measured discount — only rendered when measurement DISAGREES with the map. */
.td-redun { font-size:.7rem; color:var(--amber,#f59e0b); margin-top:.15rem; line-height:1.35; }

/* Daily no-trade / opportunity-density banner (#6). */
.td-opp { border-radius:8px; padding:9px 12px; margin:0 0 10px; border-left:4px solid var(--text-dim); background:var(--surface); }
.td-opp-head { display:flex; flex-wrap:wrap; align-items:baseline; gap:6px 12px; }
.td-opp-badge { font-size:.92rem; }
.td-opp-metrics { font-size:.74rem; color:var(--text-dim); }
.td-opp-metrics b { color:var(--text); }
.td-opp-why { margin:6px 0 2px; padding-left:18px; font-size:.72rem; line-height:1.45; }
.td-opp-avail { font-size:.7rem; margin-top:3px; }
.td-opp.op-normal { border-left-color:var(--green); }
.td-opp.op-selective { border-left-color:var(--amber,#f59e0b); }
.td-opp.op-reduced { border-left-color:#fb923c; }
.td-opp.op-notrade { border-left-color:var(--red); background:rgba(239,68,68,.06); }

/* Per-card remaining-edge (#3) — how much of the advertised move is still ahead. */
.td-remain { font-size:.7rem; margin:.15rem 0; line-height:1.35; padding:.1em .5em; border-radius:6px; }
.td-remain.re-fresh { color:var(--green); background:rgba(34,197,94,.10); }
.td-remain.re-part { color:var(--amber,#f59e0b); background:rgba(245,158,11,.10); }
.td-remain.re-late { color:var(--red); background:rgba(239,68,68,.10); }
.td-remain b { font-weight:700; }

/* ── 🎯 OMEGA ENSEMBLE (op=ensemble) ────────────────────────────────────────
   Renders the composed board: summary, portfolio caps, ranking, exclusions.
   No colour here encodes a claim the backend does not make — "not calibrated"
   is styled as dim/neutral, never as a green number. */
.oe-summary { display:grid; grid-template-columns:repeat(auto-fit,minmax(190px,1fr)); gap:10px; margin-bottom:12px; }
.oe-sum-cell { background:rgba(255,255,255,.03); border:1px solid rgba(255,255,255,.08); border-radius:10px; padding:10px 12px; }
.oe-sum-k { font-size:11px; text-transform:uppercase; letter-spacing:.06em; opacity:.6; }
.oe-sum-v { font-size:19px; font-weight:700; margin:3px 0; }
.oe-sum-sub { font-size:12px; opacity:.75; }
.oe-sum-note { font-size:11px; opacity:.55; margin-top:5px; line-height:1.35; }
.oe-good { color:#22c55e; } .oe-bad { color:#ef4444; } .oe-mid { color:#f59e0b; }
.oe-warn { background:rgba(245,158,11,.1); border:1px solid rgba(245,158,11,.3); border-radius:8px; padding:7px 10px; font-size:12px; margin-bottom:10px; }

.oe-pf { background:rgba(255,255,255,.02); border:1px solid rgba(255,255,255,.07); border-radius:10px; padding:10px 12px; margin-bottom:12px; }
.oe-pf-line { font-size:12.5px; margin:3px 0; opacity:.9; }
.oe-pf-note { font-size:11px; opacity:.55; margin-top:6px; font-style:italic; }

.oe-tablewrap { overflow-x:auto; }
.oe-table { width:100%; border-collapse:collapse; font-size:13px; }
.oe-table th { text-align:left; font-size:11px; text-transform:uppercase; letter-spacing:.05em; opacity:.6; padding:6px 8px; border-bottom:1px solid rgba(255,255,255,.12); white-space:nowrap; }
.oe-table td { padding:8px; border-bottom:1px solid rgba(255,255,255,.05); vertical-align:top; }
.oe-rank { opacity:.5; font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums; }
.oe-tk strong { font-size:14px; }
.oe-sub { font-size:11px; opacity:.55; margin-top:2px; }
.oe-score { font-weight:700; font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums; }
.oe-short { color:#ef4444; }
/* The probability column states an absence. It must never look like a value. */
.oe-prob { font-size:11.5px; opacity:.45; font-style:italic; cursor:help; }
.oe-chips { display:flex; flex-wrap:wrap; gap:4px; }
.oe-chip { display:inline-block; font-size:11px; padding:2px 6px; border-radius:5px; background:rgba(255,255,255,.06); border:1px solid rgba(255,255,255,.1); white-space:nowrap; }
.oe-chip-good { background:rgba(34,197,94,.12); border-color:rgba(34,197,94,.3); }
.oe-chip-warn { background:rgba(245,158,11,.12); border-color:rgba(245,158,11,.3); }
.oe-chip-dim { opacity:.45; }

.oe-empty { padding:18px; text-align:center; opacity:.7; font-size:13px; line-height:1.5; }
.oe-excl, .oe-disc { margin-top:12px; background:rgba(255,255,255,.02); border:1px solid rgba(255,255,255,.07); border-radius:10px; padding:8px 12px; }
.oe-excl summary, .oe-disc summary { cursor:pointer; font-size:12.5px; font-weight:600; opacity:.85; }
.oe-excl-body { margin-top:8px; }
.oe-excl-row { display:grid; grid-template-columns:64px 52px 190px 1fr; gap:8px; padding:5px 0; border-bottom:1px solid rgba(255,255,255,.04); font-size:12px; align-items:baseline; }
.oe-excl-tk { font-weight:700; }
.oe-excl-score { opacity:.6; font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums; }
.oe-excl-reason { opacity:.9; }
.oe-excl-detail { opacity:.6; font-size:11.5px; }
.oe-disc ul { margin:8px 0 2px 16px; padding:0; }
.oe-disc li { font-size:11.5px; opacity:.7; line-height:1.5; margin-bottom:5px; }
@media (max-width:720px) { .oe-excl-row { grid-template-columns:1fr; gap:2px; } }

``````

## public/index.html

``````html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8" />
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0" />
  <title>Market Intelligence</title>
  <link rel="manifest" href="/manifest.webmanifest" />
  <meta name="theme-color" content="#060b14" />
  <link rel="icon" href="/icon.svg" type="image/svg+xml" />
  <link rel="apple-touch-icon" href="/icon.svg" />
  <meta name="mobile-web-app-capable" content="yes" />
  <meta name="apple-mobile-web-app-capable" content="yes" />
  <meta name="apple-mobile-web-app-status-bar-style" content="black-translucent" />
  <meta name="apple-mobile-web-app-title" content="Signals" />
  <link rel="stylesheet" href="/css/app.css">
</head>
<body>

<header>
  <div class="logo">
    <div class="logo-icon">M</div>
    <div>
      <div class="logo-text">Market Intelligence</div>
    </div>
  </div>

  <nav class="header-nav">
    <a class="nav-link" href="#today" data-tab="home">🏠 Today</a>
    <a class="nav-link" href="#opportunities" data-tab="candidates">🎯 Candidates</a>
    <a class="nav-link" href="#coremo" data-tab="positions">💼 Portfolio</a>
    <a class="nav-link" href="#rotation" data-tab="markets">📊 Markets</a>
    <a class="nav-link" href="#gameplan" data-tab="predict">🔮 Predict</a>
    <a class="nav-link" href="#scoreboard" data-tab="proof">🎖️ Evidence</a>
    <a class="nav-link" href="#events" data-tab="lab">🔬 Research Lab</a>
  </nav>

  <div class="header-right">
    <div class="market-status" id="market-status">
      <div class="dot-live"></div>
      <span id="market-status-text">–</span>
    </div>
    <span id="live-clock"></span>
    <button class="hdr-btn" id="mode-btn" title="Simple hides the dense stats & research; Expert shows everything">🌱 Simple</button>
    <button class="hdr-btn" id="starthere-btn" title="New here? The plain-English beginner's guide to using this app">📘 Start Here</button>
    <button class="hdr-btn" id="learn-btn" title="Learn every concept — novice to expert">📚 Learn</button>
    <button class="hdr-btn" id="refresh-btn">⟳ Refresh</button>
  </div>
</header>

<nav class="mobile-top-tabs" id="mobile-top-tabs">
  <a class="mtt-item" href="#today" data-tab="home"><span class="mtt-ic">🏠</span>Today</a>
  <a class="mtt-item" href="#opportunities" data-tab="candidates"><span class="mtt-ic">🎯</span>Candidates</a>
  <a class="mtt-item" href="#coremo" data-tab="positions"><span class="mtt-ic">💼</span>Portfolio</a>
  <a class="mtt-item" href="#rotation" data-tab="markets"><span class="mtt-ic">📊</span>Markets</a>
  <a class="mtt-item" href="#gameplan" data-tab="predict"><span class="mtt-ic">🔮</span>Predict</a>
  <a class="mtt-item" href="#scoreboard" data-tab="proof"><span class="mtt-ic">🎖️</span>Evidence</a>
  <a class="mtt-item" href="#events" data-tab="lab"><span class="mtt-ic">🔬</span>Research Lab</a>
</nav>

<div class="page">

  <div id="hub-subnav"></div>
  <div id="trust-badge" style="display:none"></div>
  <div id="tape-badge" style="display:none"></div>

  <!-- 🏆 ALGO LEADERBOARD — top screener strategies by realized 3-month performance -->
  <section class="scoreboard-section" id="leaderboard">
    <div class="section-label">
      <div class="accent-bar" style="background:#f0a832"></div>
      <h2 style="color:#f0a832">🏆 Algo Leaderboard</h2>
      <span class="label-meta">· the app's screener strategies ranked by realized performance</span>
      <div class="label-action">
        <button class="refresh-btn" style="color:#f0a832" id="leaderboard-refresh-btn">⟳ Refresh</button>
      </div>
    </div>
    <div id="leaderboard-container">
      <div class="mom-status"><div class="mom-spinner"></div><p>Ranking the screener algos…</p></div>
    </div>
  </section>

  <!-- SIGNAL SCOREBOARD -->
  <section class="scoreboard-section" id="scoreboard">
    <div class="section-label">
      <div class="accent-bar" style="background:#f0a832"></div>
      <h2 style="color:#f0a832">Signal Scoreboard</h2>
      <span class="label-meta" id="scoreboard-meta">· realized forward returns by section &amp; tier — which signals actually pay</span>
      <div class="label-action">
        <span class="lbl-time" id="scoreboard-gen-time"></span>
        <button class="refresh-btn" style="color:#f0a832" id="scoreboard-refresh-btn">⟳ Refresh</button>
      </div>
    </div>
    <details class="sb-help">
      <summary>ℹ️ How the scoreboard works</summary>
      <div class="sb-help-body">
        <p>Every ticker the <b>Screener</b> and <b>Momentum</b> sections surface is logged once a day with its entry price. The numbers below are <b>realized</b> forward returns pulled from actual price history — nothing is simulated.</p>

        <h4>Reading a card</h4>
        <ul>
          <li><b>Expectancy badge</b> (Positive / Negative / Pending) — the headline verdict, based on the <b>1-month</b> average return (falls back to 1-week, then 3-month until it matures).</li>
          <li><b>1-Week / 1-Month / 3-Month</b> — average return, win rate, and sample size (<code>n</code>) for each horizon.</li>
          <li><b>Avg win / Avg loss</b> — typical size of winners vs losers.</li>
        </ul>

        <h4>How it's counted</h4>
        <ul>
          <li>Each signal is scored on its <b>first appearance</b>, so names that linger for days aren't over-counted.</li>
          <li><b>Strong Sell</b> returns are <b>inverted</b> — positive means the short worked (price fell).</li>
          <li>Treat small samples (<code>n</code> under ~15–20) as noise, not signal.</li>
        </ul>

        <h4>Turning signals off</h4>
        <ul>
          <li>Click <b>✓ Enabled / ✕ Disabled</b> to hide a tier from its section (e.g. hide "Setup" from the Screener). It keeps being tracked so you can re-enable it if it recovers. Saved on this device only.</li>
        </ul>

        <h4>When numbers appear</h4>
        <ul>
          <li>Tracking is <b>forward-only</b> — it can't backfill picks from before it was switched on.</li>
          <li>1-Week fills after ~5 trading days, 1-Month after ~21, 3-Month after ~63. Until then a horizon reads "pending."</li>
          <li>Picks are logged automatically each weekday by the daily cron (the snapshot write is authenticated, so it isn't triggerable from the browser).</li>
        </ul>
      </div>
    </details>

    <div id="scoreboard-container">
      <div class="mom-status"><div class="mom-spinner"></div><p>Loading tracked picks…</p></div>
    </div>
  </section>

  <!-- 🎖️ EVIDENCE — maturity grade of every strategy, earned from its own track record -->
  <section class="scoreboard-section" id="evidence">
    <div class="section-label">
      <div class="accent-bar" style="background:#22c55e"></div>
      <h2 style="color:#22c55e">🎖️ Evidence &amp; Maturity</h2>
      <span class="label-meta">· how much to trust each strategy — a grade earned from its own resolved track record</span>
      <div class="label-action">
        <span class="lbl-time" id="evidence-gen-time"></span>
        <button class="refresh-btn" style="color:#22c55e" id="evidence-refresh-btn">⟳ Refresh</button>
      </div>
    </div>
    <div id="evidence-container">
      <div class="mom-status"><div class="mom-spinner"></div><p>Grading strategies…</p></div>
    </div>
  </section>

  <!-- 🧪 BASELINES — does each strategy beat a dumb baseline? -->
  <section class="scoreboard-section" id="baselines">
    <div class="section-label">
      <div class="accent-bar" style="background:#38bdf8"></div>
      <h2 style="color:#38bdf8">🧪 Baseline Validation</h2>
      <span class="label-meta">· does each strategy beat SPY, its sector, a random pick, and simple momentum / 52-week / rel-volume screens?</span>
      <div class="label-action">
        <span class="lbl-time" id="baselines-gen-time"></span>
        <button class="refresh-btn" style="color:#38bdf8" id="baselines-refresh-btn">⟳ Refresh</button>
      </div>
    </div>
    <div id="baselines-container">
      <div class="mom-status"><div class="mom-spinner"></div><p>Assembling baselines…</p></div>
    </div>
  </section>

  <!-- ⭐ OPPORTUNITIES — ranked pre-breakout buys (the "what to buy" centerpiece) -->

Where ⭐ Opportunities shows one cap size at a time, Quick Hit shows the best of all three together.</p>
        <p><b>When to use it.</b> When you have 30 seconds and want the highest-conviction handful without clicking through each screener.</p>

  <!-- CORE MOMENTUM (survivorship-safe small/mid sector-neutral 12-1) -->
  <section class="screener-section" id="coremo">
    <div class="section-label">
      <div class="accent-bar" style="background:#10d98a"></div>
      <h2 style="color:#10d98a">Core Momentum</h2>
      <span class="label-meta" id="coremo-meta">· survivorship-safe small/mid sector-neutral 12-1 · quarterly</span>
      <div class="label-action">
        <span class="lbl-time" id="coremo-gen-time"></span>
        <button class="refresh-btn" style="color:#10d98a" id="coremo-refresh-btn">⟳ Refresh</button>
      </div>
    </div>

    <details class="coremo-howto" style="margin:4px 0 12px;border:1px solid #1d2735;border-left:3px solid #10d98a;border-radius:8px;background:#0e1622;overflow:hidden">
      <summary style="cursor:pointer;padding:10px 14px;font-weight:600;color:#10d98a;list-style:none">ℹ️ New here? How to use this screener — start with this</summary>
      <div style="padding:2px 16px 14px;font-size:0.82rem;line-height:1.5;color:#cbd2dd">
        <p><b>What is this?</b> A ready-made list of small and mid-sized US stocks that have been climbing steadily for about a year. Decades of research show stocks already trending up tend to keep trending up for a while — that pattern is called <b>momentum</b>. This tab finds the strongest ones and strips out the riskiest.</p>

        <p style="margin-top:10px"><b>What am I looking at?</b> A table of ~100 stocks. Think of it as <b>one basket to buy together</b>, not a menu to cherry-pick from. Each row shows the ticker, its industry, how much it has risen over the year, and a reference price.</p>

        <p style="margin-top:10px"><b>How to use it — 4 simple steps</b></p>
        <ol style="margin:4px 0 0;padding-left:20px">
          <li><b>Buy the whole list in equal amounts.</b> Spreading evenly across all the names is what makes it work — don't just buy your favourites. (Investing $10,000? That's about $100 in each.)</li>
          <li><b>Hold for about 3 months.</b> This is not day-trading. Give the stocks time to do their thing.</li>
          <li><b>Re-check every 3 months</b> (January, April, July, October). Sell names that have dropped off the list, buy the new ones, keep the rest. Names you'd already own are marked with a green ● dot.</li>
          <li><b>Don't use tight stop-losses.</b> These stocks are bouncy day-to-day; bailing out on every dip loses money here. The plan is simply to hold for the quarter.</li>
        </ol>

        <p style="margin-top:10px"><b>How much should I put in?</b> Treat this as <b>one small slice</b> of your overall portfolio, not all of it. Small/mid-cap stocks swing hard. Keep most of your money in broad, diversified funds and use this as a smaller "tilt".</p>

        <p style="margin-top:10px"><b>How do I know it's working?</b></p>
        <ul style="margin:4px 0 0;padding-left:20px">
          <li>The coloured <b>health badge</b> at the top grades it once enough trades finish: <span style="color:#10d98a">green = healthy</span>, <span style="color:#f0a832">yellow = easing off</span>, <span style="color:#ef4444">red = stop using it</span>.</li>
          <li>The <b>📈 Core Performance</b> tab (under 🏆 Track) shows its real quarterly results vs the small-cap market (IWM). Trust that over the theory.</li>
        </ul>

        <p style="margin-top:10px;color:#8a93a6"><b>Honest expectations.</b> This is a <b>modest, real edge — not a get-rich scheme.</b> It does best when the overall market is rising and can lose money when the market falls. Expect ups and downs, and judge it over a year or more, not a few weeks. Research, not financial advice.</p>
      </div>
    </details>

    <div id="coremo-health-banner"></div>
    <div id="coremo-container">
      <div class="mom-status"><div class="mom-spinner"></div><p>Open this tab to load the Core Momentum book…</p></div>
    </div>
  </section>

  <!-- CERN — Causal Event-Response Network -->
  <section class="screener-section" id="events">
    <div class="section-label">
      <div class="accent-bar" style="background:#ffd23f"></div>
      <h2 style="color:#ffd23f">Events · CERN</h2>
      <span class="label-meta" id="events-meta">· forced-flow event engine (self-learning)</span>
      <div class="label-action">
        <span class="lbl-time" id="events-gen-time"></span>
        <button class="refresh-btn" style="color:#06c4d4" id="ev-help-btn">ℹ️ How to use</button>
        <button class="refresh-btn" style="color:#ffd23f" id="events-refresh-btn">⟳ Refresh</button>
      </div>
    </div>
    <div id="events-container">
      <div class="mom-status"><div class="mom-spinner"></div><p>Open this tab to load the event engine…</p></div>
    </div>
  </section>

  <!-- CERN — How to use -->
  <div class="cx-help-backdrop" id="ev-help-modal" hidden>
    <div class="cx-help-card" role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-label="How to use the Events tab">
      <button class="cx-help-x" id="ev-help-close" aria-label="Close">✕</button>
      <h2>⚡ CERN — forced-flow events</h2>
      <p class="cx-help-lead">A different bet from every other tab. The screeners hunt <i>momentum</i>; CERN hunts <b>forced flow</b> — moments when someone <i>has</i> to trade regardless of price (a fund dumping a deleted index name, tax-loss selling in December, a margin spiral). Forced selling overshoots, then reverts. CERN measures the dislocation, checks the flow is <b>uninformed</b> (no news/earnings/attention behind it), and learns how much reverts.</p>

      <div class="cx-help-rule">⭐ <b>This is a learning system first, a trading system second.</b> It logs and resolves <b>every</b> qualifying event whether it trades or not — so it learns from 100% of the world while risking capital on a fraction. Months 0–3 will be mostly <b>LOG_ONLY / PROBE</b> (paper). That isn't it failing — that's the archive filling and the model earning its confidence.</div>

      <h3>🎯 The three decision types</h3>
      <ul class="cx-help-ul">
        <li><b>TRADE</b> — passed every gate (completion, absorption, posterior P(profit)≥65%, ship-gate) and is sized with ¼-Kelly. Rare early.</li>
        <li><b>PROBE</b> — a paper position the engine takes to <i>learn</i> a promising-but-uncertain event type (Thompson-sampled exploration budget).</li>
        <li><b>LOG_ONLY</b> — logged and resolved for learning, no position. Watching these resolve is how you learn to trust (or distrust) it.</li>
      </ul>

      <h3>🧠 The posteriors panel</h3>
      <p class="cx-help-p">Each event type shows <b>κ (kappa)</b> — the fraction of the dislocation it has learned reverts — with its uncertainty (±sd) and the number of resolved events (n). High κ + low sd + high n = a trusted edge. A <b>drifted</b> type (struck through) means the world changed and the engine auto-shrank its sizing until the evidence re-tightens. <b>Candidate</b> alerts are recurring unexplained reversions the engine wants you to name as new event types.</p>

      <h3>🚫 What to keep in mind</h3>
      <ul class="cx-help-ul">
        <li><b>Real size is gated</b> behind a posterior t-stat ≥ 2 per event type — it will not bet big on a hunch.</li>
        <li><b>All seven types are live</b>: margin spirals + tax-loss selling (auto-detected from price/volume), plus index add/delete, lockup expiries, fund fire-sales and sell-side downgrades (calendar/flow/ratings feeds).</li>
        <li><b>Fire-sales &amp; downgrades are proxies</b> (honest): real ETF creation/redemption flow isn't on our data tier, so a fund's heavy-volume drawdown stands in for redemptions; and an analyst downgrade is a softer, part-sentiment "forced flow." In both cases the gates + counterfactual logging sort the signal from the noise.</li>
        <li><b>Two data gaps</b> (honest): attention is proxied by a volume spike, and estimate-revisions aren't wired yet — so the "uninformed" check is good but not perfect.</li>
      </ul>
      <p class="cx-help-foot">The moat is the dataset this builds, not any single trade. Let it log, watch the κ posteriors move, and trust it only once they've earned it.</p>
    </div>
  </div>

  <section class="screener-section" id="start">
    <div class="section-label">
      <div class="accent-bar" style="background:#34c759"></div>
      <h2 style="color:#34c759">📘 Start Here — A Beginner's Guide</h2>
      <span class="label-meta">· how to use this app to find stock picks, in plain English</span>
    </div>

    <div class="bt-note">
      <b>New to investing? Read this first.</b> This app is a <b>research dashboard</b> — it scans the market and ranks stocks using data-driven signals so you don't have to stare at charts all day. It is <b>not</b> a crystal ball, a tip service, or financial advice. Used well, it helps you find <i>better-than-random</i> candidates and avoid obvious mistakes. That's it — and that's still valuable. Below is the simplest way to use it.
    </div>

    <div class="bt-eff">
      <div class="bt-eff-head">🧭 4 honest truths before you risk a single dollar</div>
      <div class="bt-eff-sub" style="line-height:1.7">
        <b>1. No signal is a sure thing.</b> The best edges here are <i>modest</i> — they tilt the odds slightly in your favor, like a casino's house edge, not a winning lottery ticket. You win by making <b>many small, sensible bets</b>, not one big "sure" one.<br>
        <b>2. The market's "weather" matters more than any single pick.</b> When the overall market is falling, almost everything goes down with it. Avoiding bad conditions is the most powerful thing you can do.<br>
        <b>3. Only invest money you can afford to lose.</b> Never rent money, emergency savings, or money you need within a few years.<br>
        <b>4. Practice first.</b> "Paper trade" (track picks on paper or in a free simulator) for a few months before using real money. See if it actually works <i>for you</i>.
      </div>
    </div>

    <div class="bt-eff">
      <div class="bt-eff-head">🌦️ Step 1 — Check the market "weather" (the regime)</div>
      <div class="bt-eff-sub" style="line-height:1.7">
        Look for the <b>regime</b> indicator (shown on the <b>📓 Edge Book</b> and <b>Markets</b> tabs). Think of it as a traffic light:<br>
        🟢 <b>Risk-on</b> = green light. The market is healthy; it's a reasonable time to look for stocks to buy.<br>
        🟡 <b>Neutral</b> = yellow light. Proceed carefully, smaller bets.<br>
        🔴 <b>Risk-off</b> = red light. The market is stressed. The app's own research shows buying here usually <i>loses</i> — the smartest move is mostly to <b>stay out and wait</b>. This one rule is the single biggest thing separating good results from bad.
      </div>
    </div>

    <div class="bt-eff">
      <div class="bt-eff-head">📝 Step 2 — Get your shortlist</div>
      <div class="bt-eff-sub" style="line-height:1.7">
        The easiest starting point is the <b>📓 Edge Book → "Sleeve A · Conviction longs."</b> That's a short, ranked list of the strongest names the app sees right now (already filtered by the green/yellow/red light above). Start there.<br><br>
        Want to explore more? Other tabs are just different "lenses" on the market:<br>
        • <b>📊 Markets</b> — stocks breaking out to new highs with strength.<br>
        • <b>🧠 Momentum</b> — names ranked by trend and quality.<br>
        • <b>👻 Ghost Accumulation</b> — quiet stocks that may be getting bought before a big move.<br>
        <b>How to read any of them:</b> most tabs sort names into <b>tiers</b> (or give a <b>score</b>). Higher tier / higher score = stronger signal. <b>Start at the top of the list, never the bottom.</b>
      </div>
    </div>

    <div class="bt-eff">
      <div class="bt-eff-head">🤝 Step 3 — Prefer agreement (this is the secret)</div>
      <div class="bt-eff-sub" style="line-height:1.7">
        The highest-quality picks are the ones that show up as <b>strong in more than one place</b>. If a stock is top-ranked in the Edge Book <i>and</i> shows up in Ghost Accumulation <i>and</i> has insiders buying — that agreement is far more meaningful than any one signal alone. When several independent signals point at the same name, that's your best setup. When they disagree, it's usually best to skip it.
      </div>
    </div>

    <div class="bt-eff">
      <div class="bt-eff-head">🛡️ Step 4 — Protect your money (the part most beginners skip)</div>
      <div class="bt-eff-sub" style="line-height:1.7">
        • <b>Spread it out.</b> Don't put everything in one stock. ~10–15 names means one bad pick won't sink you.<br>
        • <b>Size each bet small.</b> A common rule: risk no more than 1–2% of your total on any single stock.<br>
        • <b>Decide your exit before you buy.</b> Know the price where you'll admit you were wrong and sell. Small losses are survivable; big ones aren't.<br>
        • <b>Don't chase.</b> If you missed it, let it go — there's always another setup. FOMO (fear of missing out) is expensive.
      </div>
    </div>

    <div class="bt-eff">
      <div class="bt-eff-head">🏆 Step 5 — Keep the app honest</div>
      <div class="bt-eff-sub" style="line-height:1.7">
        Visit the <b>🏆 Scoreboard</b> tab. It tracks how the app's <i>past</i> picks actually performed — win rates and returns by signal. Before you trust any signal with real money, check whether it has actually been working. If a section's track record is weak, weight it less. This app is built to be held accountable — use that.
      </div>
    </div>

    <div class="bt-eff">
      <div class="bt-eff-head">📖 Plain-English dictionary</div>
      <div class="bt-eff-sub" style="line-height:1.8">
        <b>Ticker</b> — a stock's short code (Apple = AAPL).<br>
        <b>Momentum</b> — a stock that's already trending up; the app leans on the idea that strength tends to continue (for a while).<br>
        <b>Regime</b> — the overall market mood (risk-on / neutral / risk-off). The "weather."<br>
        <b>Conviction score</b> — the app's combined 0–100 rating of how strong a stock looks. Higher = stronger.<br>
        <b>Tier</b> — a quality bucket (e.g. top tier vs watchlist). Higher tier = stronger signal.<br>
        <b>Long</b> — betting a stock goes <i>up</i> (the normal "buy"). <b>Short</b> — betting it goes <i>down</i> (advanced; skip as a beginner).<br>
        <b>Diversify</b> — spread money across many stocks so no single one can ruin you.<br>
        <b>Stop-loss</b> — a pre-decided price where you sell to cap a loss.<br>
        <b>Paper trading</b> — practicing with pretend money to test a strategy risk-free.
      </div>
    </div>

    <div class="chart-disclaimer" style="margin-top:14px">
      ⚠️ <b>Important:</b> This app is an educational research tool, <b>not financial advice</b>, and nothing here is a recommendation to buy or sell. The signals carry only modest, uncertain edges, and past performance never guarantees future results. Investing involves real risk of loss. Do your own research and consider speaking with a licensed financial advisor before investing real money.
    </div>
  </section>

  <!-- EDGE BOOK — two orthogonal sleeves: conviction longs + CERN forced-flow -->
  <section class="screener-section" id="edge">
    <div class="section-label">

    </div>
  </section>

  <!-- OVERHEATED (self-improving inverted-V fade engine) -->
  <section class="screener-section" id="fade">
    <div class="section-label">
      <div class="accent-bar" style="background:#ff6b4a"></div>
      <h2 style="color:#ff6b4a">🔥 Overheated</h2>
      <span class="label-meta" id="fade-meta">· names that spiked and are rolling over — avoid chasing / consider trimming</span>
      <div class="label-action">
        <span class="lbl-time" id="fade-gen-time"></span>
        <button class="refresh-btn" style="color:#ff6b4a" id="fade-refresh-btn">⟳ Refresh</button>
      </div>
    </div>
    <details class="scr-help">
      <summary>📖 New here? What is Overheated?</summary>
      <div class="scr-help-body">
        <p><b>What this is — the <i>opposite</i> of a buy list.</b> Names that <b>spiked too far and are now rolling over</b> (an "inverted-V" — sharp run up, then turning down). This is a <b>caution</b> screen, not a shopping list.</p>
        <p class="scr-help-h">What it helps you with:</p>
        <ul>
          <li><b>Avoid chasing.</b> If a name you were about to buy shows up here, it's already extended and fading — think twice.</li>
          <li><b>Protect gains.</b> If you <b>own</b> one of these, it's a heads-up to consider trimming or tightening your stop.</li>
          <li><b>Advanced:</b> experienced traders use these as short/fade candidates — higher risk.</li>
        </ul>
        <p class="scr-help-note">Being on this list is a ⚠ warning, not a buy signal. Research, not advice.</p>
      </div>
    </details>

    <div id="fade-container">
      <div class="mom-status"><div class="mom-spinner"></div><p>Open this tab to load Overheated names…</p></div>
    </div>
  </section>

  <!-- TREND RIDER (trend-following + momentum + market-climate traffic light) -->
  <section class="screener-section" id="trendrider">
    <div class="section-label">
      <div class="accent-bar" style="background:#22c55e"></div>
      <h2 style="color:#22c55e">🚦 Trend Rider</h2>
      <span class="label-meta" id="trendr-meta">· ride trends when the light is green · stand down on red</span>
      <div class="label-action">
        <span class="lbl-time" id="trendr-gen-time"></span>
        <button class="refresh-btn" style="color:#22c55e" id="trendr-refresh-btn">⟳ Refresh</button>
      </div>
    </div>
    <details class="scr-help">
      <summary>📖 New here? What is Trend Rider?</summary>
      <div class="scr-help-body">
        <p><b>What this is.</b> A trend-following list with a <b>market "traffic light."</b> 🟢 Green = conditions favor riding trends; 🔴 Red = the broad market is shaky, so stand down. It finds stocks already in strong, <b>established</b> uptrends.</p>
        <p><b>What it helps you find.</b> Existing winners to <b>ride</b>, rather than early movers to catch. The difference from the Breakout/Emerging tabs: those get you in early; this rides trends that are already proven — but only when the light says it's safe.</p>
        <p class="scr-help-h">How to use it:</p>
        <ol>
          <li><b>Check the light first.</b> Green → look at the names. <b>Red → sit on your hands</b> (the light keeps you out of bad markets — that's its main job).</li>
          <li>On green, read each card's <b>Entry / Stop / Target</b> and confirm on a chart.</li>
          <li><b>Size by risk</b> and mind the <b>⚠ liquidity badge</b>.</li>
        </ol>
        <p class="scr-help-note">The light is the discipline — don't override a red. Research, not advice.</p>
      </div>
    </details>

    <div id="trendr-container">
      <div class="mom-status"><div class="mom-spinner"></div><p>Open this tab to load the market-climate light…</p></div>
    </div>
  </section>

      <div class="label-action">
        <span class="lbl-time" id="aligned-gen-time"></span>
        <button class="refresh-btn" style="color:#0ea5a5" id="aligned-refresh-btn">⟳ Refresh</button>
      </div>
    </div>
    <p class="scr-help-note">Drawn from the breakout-screener pool, then kept only where the short-term signal AND the long-term trend read both say buy (trend-continuation). Research to help you find candidates — not financial advice.</p>
    <div id="aligned-container">
      <div class="mom-status"><div class="mom-spinner"></div><p>Open this tab to scan for names aligned on both horizons…</p></div>

A recent-window pilot found these FADE (offering 21d +0%, buyout −5.7%) while FDA/guidance/contract gaps continue (+5%). NOT confirmed (single regime, small n) — the forward ledger accrues toward ~150/class before this is trusted. Off by default.">🩳 Skip fades</button>

  <!-- CONFLUENCE (5 classic strategies agree, self-learning) -->
  <section class="screener-section" id="confluence">
    <div class="section-label">
      <div class="accent-bar" style="background:#06c4d4"></div>
      <h2 style="color:#06c4d4">⚙️ Confluence</h2>
      <span class="label-meta" id="cfl-meta">· 5 classic strategies agree · self-learning</span>
      <div class="label-action">
        <span class="lbl-time" id="cfl-gen-time"></span>
        <button class="refresh-btn" style="color:#06c4d4" id="cfl-refresh-btn">⟳ Refresh</button>
      </div>
    </div>
    <details class="scr-help">
      <summary>📖 New here? What is Confluence?</summary>
      <div class="scr-help-body">
        <p><b>What this is.</b> It runs <b>5 classic strategies</b> on every stock — moving-average trend, Supertrend, RSI, MACD, and price-action — and surfaces the names where <b>most of them agree</b>. "Confluence" just means several independent signals pointing the same way at once.</p>
        <p><b>What it helps you find.</b> Setups backed by <b>more than one</b> indicator, not a single lucky signal — generally a sturdier, higher-quality buy idea. The tab is <b>self-learning</b>: it tracks which strategies have actually been working lately and leans on those.</p>
        <p class="scr-help-h">How to use it:</p>
        <ol>
          <li>Favor names where the <b>most strategies agree</b> — that's the whole point of the tab.</li>
          <li>Read the card's <b>Entry / Stop / Target</b> and confirm on a chart.</li>
          <li><b>Size by risk</b> and check the <b>⚠ liquidity badge</b>.</li>
        </ol>
        <p class="scr-help-note">More agreement = stronger signal, not a guarantee. Research, not advice.</p>
      </div>
    </details>

    <div id="cfl-container">
      <div class="mom-status"><div class="mom-spinner"></div><p>Open this tab to run the multi-strategy scan…</p></div>
    </div>
  </section>

  <!-- How-to-use guide modal -->
  <!-- 📚 Learn glossary modal (teaching layer: every concept, novice → expert) -->
  <div class="cx-help-backdrop" id="learn-modal" hidden>
    <div class="cx-help-card" role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-label="Learn the concepts">
      <button class="cx-help-x" id="learn-close" aria-label="Close">✕</button>
      <h2>📚 Learn — from novice to expert</h2>
      <p class="cx-help-lead">Every concept this app uses, in plain English — <b>what it is</b>, <b>why it matters</b>, and <b>how pros use it</b>. Tap any <span class="learn-term">dotted term</span> anywhere in the app to jump straight here. Read a few each day and you'll be reading the market like a pro.</p>
      <div id="learn-body"></div>
    </div>
  </div>

  <div class="cx-help-backdrop" id="cx-help-modal" hidden>
    <div class="cx-help-card" role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-label="How to use this app">
      <button class="cx-help-x" id="cx-help-close" aria-label="Close">✕</button>
      <h2>How to get the most out of this app</h2>
      <p class="cx-help-lead">A momentum &amp; market-regime dashboard. Used honestly, it's a strong co-pilot for your decisions — not an autopilot, and not a money printer. Here's how to actually benefit.</p>

      <div class="cx-help-rule">⭐ <b>The one rule that matters most:</b> respect the regime badge at the top of this tab. <b style="color:var(--green)">Risk-On</b> = hunt. <b style="color:var(--amber)">Neutral</b> = be selective, size down. <b style="color:var(--red)">Risk-Off</b> = stand down on new long trades — this is the clearest, most repeatable finding in all our back-testing.</div>

      <h3>⏱ The 2-minute daily routine</h3>
      <ol class="cx-help-ol">
        <li><b>Check the regime badge first.</b> If it's red, the best move today is usually <i>not trading</i>.</li>
        <li><b>Glance at Markets → Sector Rotation</b> to see where money is flowing in vs. out; lean toward leading sectors.</li>
        <li><b>Only then</b> look at individual names.</li>
      </ol>

      <h3>🧭 What each tab is for</h3>
      <ul class="cx-help-ul">
        <li><b>🧠 Custom Screener</b> — your workspace. <b>Apex / Loaded</b> tiers are a <i>shortlist of strong names to research</i>, not buy signals. Read the pillar bars to see <i>why</i> a name scored. Use the <b>📊 Portfolio panel</b> for disciplined position sizing &amp; concentration warnings.</li>
        <li><b>📊 Markets</b> — Breakout Screener (idea generation), Sector Rotation (where money flows), Sectors (today's leaders), Backtest (expectations).</li>
        <li><b>🏆 Scoreboard</b> — your reality check. Open it weekly; let it (not your memory) tell you which signals are actually working, and disable the ones that aren't.</li>
        <li><b>🔥 Alerts</b> — real-time momentum flips; good for <i>timing</i> a name you already like.</li>
        <li><b>⭐ Picks · ⚡ Options · 📰 News</b> — context to <i>understand</i> a name, not to decide on one.</li>
      </ul>

      <h3>🚫 What not to do (lessons the back-testing paid for)</h3>
      <ul class="cx-help-ul">
        <li>Don't take new long trades in <b style="color:var(--red)">Risk-Off</b>.</li>
        <li>Don't treat "Apex" as a guaranteed winner — it's a strong candidate in a good tape, nothing more.</li>
        <li>Don't over-concentrate; heed the sector-concentration warning.</li>
        <li>Don't bet big expecting alpha — the app has no proven standalone edge, so size with humility.</li>
        <li>Don't go unhedged into a downturn just because tight stops sometimes whipsaw.</li>
      </ul>

      <p class="cx-help-foot">Stay on the right side of the regime, size with discipline, and let the Scoreboard keep you honest. That's how you benefit from this app.</p>
    </div>
  </div>

  <!-- GHOST — How to use -->
  <div class="cx-help-backdrop" id="gh-help-modal" hidden>
    <div class="cx-help-card" role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-label="How to use the Ghost Accumulation tab">
      <button class="cx-help-x" id="gh-help-close" aria-label="Close">✕</button>
      <h2>👻 Ghost Accumulation — how to use it</h2>
      <p class="cx-help-lead">Ghost looks for the <i>opposite</i> of the Momentum tab. Where Apex hunts <b>confirmed breakouts</b>, Ghost hunts names being <b>quietly accumulated <i>before</i></b> the move — rising relative strength on heavy buying, a tightening supply vacuum, and (the new lever) <b>insider buying</b>.</p>

      <div class="cx-help-rule">⭐ <b>Same one rule:</b> respect the regime badge. <b style="color:var(--green)">Risk-On</b> = these accumulation setups are worth hunting. <b style="color:var(--red)">Risk-Off</b> = the kill switch downgrades every tier a notch and you should stand down on new longs — accumulation signals fail in ugly tapes too.</div>

      <h3>🧠 Momentum vs 👻 Ghost — when to use which</h3>
      <p class="cx-help-p">Both tabs read the same universe, and a strong name in a good tape will often show up in both. The simplest way to hold the difference in your head — it's about <b>where on the timeline</b> you're catching a move:</p>
      <div class="cx-cmp">
        <div class="cx-cmp-col">
          <div class="cx-cmp-h" style="color:#f0a832">🧠 Momentum</div>
          <div class="cx-cmp-q">"Is this breakout worth buying <i>now</i>?"</div>
          <ul class="cx-help-ul">
            <li>Fires <b>after</b> the breakout confirms</li>
            <li>Requires a confirmed setup + no weak pillar</li>
            <li>Pure price / volume / fundamentals</li>
          </ul>
        </div>
        <div class="cx-cmp-col">
          <div class="cx-cmp-h" style="color:#9b8cff">👻 Ghost</div>
          <div class="cx-cmp-q">"Is something being bought <i>before</i> it breaks out?"</div>
          <ul class="cx-help-ul">
            <li>Fires <b>earlier</b> — no breakout required</li>
            <li>Needs breadth (≥3 strong pillars)</li>
            <li>Adds <b>insider buying</b> — data the chart can't show</li>
          </ul>
        </div>
      </div>
      <p class="cx-help-p">Where they <b>diverge</b> — the names worth noticing:</p>
      <ul class="cx-help-ul">
        <li><b>Insider cluster-buying but middling momentum</b> → Ghost flags it, Momentum ignores it.</li>
        <li><b>Mid-accumulation, not yet broken out</b> → Ghost <i>Stalking</i>, Momentum shows nothing.</li>
        <li><b>Clean breakout that already ran</b> → Momentum <i>Loaded</i>, Ghost rates it lower (the quiet accumulation is already spent).</li>
      </ul>
      <p class="cx-help-p" style="color:var(--text-dim)">Honest note: because both lean on relative strength and share the same candidate pool, expect meaningful overlap. The real value of Ghost is the <b>earlier timing</b> and the <b>insider signal</b> — and whether that timing edge is real is exactly what the pick ledger is being built to measure.</p>

      <h3>🎯 How to read a Ghost card</h3>
      <ul class="cx-help-ul">
        <li><b>Score /100 &amp; tier</b> — <b>👻 Ghost</b> = score ≥80 with ≥3 strong pillars (broad accumulation); <b>🥷 Stalking</b> = ≥65, ≥2 strong (building); <b>👁 Watch</b> = ≥50 (early footprints). Higher tier = more pillars agree, not "more likely to go up."</li>
        <li><b>The 6 pillar bars</b> tell you <i>why</i> it scored — read them, don't just trust the number.</li>
        <li><b>The 🏛 Insider line</b> is the signal Ghost adds over every other tab: real open-market insider buys/sells over 90 days, with a <b>cluster-buy</b> flag when multiple insiders are buying. A green net with a cluster flag is the strongest version.</li>
        <li><b>Entry / Stop / Target</b> come from the same level engine as the other tabs — a starting framework, not a mandate.</li>
      </ul>

      <h3>🧱 The six pillars</h3>
      <ul class="cx-help-ul">
        <li><b>① Relative strength</b> — quietly outperforming the index. <i>The most validated factor.</i></li>
        <li><b>② Accumulation footprint</b> — up-days-on-volume; buyers in control.</li>
        <li><b>③ Accumulation vacuum</b> — tight, coiled base on drying volume. <b>Deliberately weighted lowest</b> — our own research found this kind of base/dry-up signal has ~zero edge, so don't lean on it.</li>
        <li><b>④ Smart-money flow</b> — up/down volume + volatility-adjusted momentum.</li>
        <li><b>⑤ Catalyst overlay</b> — real revenue/EPS acceleration + narrative + an earnings catalyst on the horizon.</li>
        <li><b>⑥ Insider buying</b> — the new, still-unproven lever. Promising, not gospel.</li>
      </ul>

      <h3>🚫 What to keep in mind</h3>
      <ul class="cx-help-ul">
        <li><b>It re-ranks the breakout pool, not the whole market (yet).</b> Ghost scores the same candidates the screener surfaces, through an accumulation lens. On a quiet large-cap day it can legitimately show <i>nothing</i> — switch the scope to Small caps, or just check back.</li>
        <li><b>The weights are static priors.</b> The self-learning engine that tunes them is a later phase; it won't even activate until ~40 of these picks have resolved (≈2–3 months). Until then, treat Ghost as a hypothesis generator.</li>
        <li><b>Insider buying is informational.</b> It's the reason Ghost exists, but it has not yet been validated on this app's data — size accordingly.</li>
        <li>Everything from the main guide still applies: respect the regime, size with discipline, let the Scoreboard keep you honest.</li>
      </ul>

      <p class="cx-help-foot">Use Ghost to find names being <i>bought before they move</i>, then confirm the story yourself. It's an early-warning radar, not a buy button.</p>
    </div>
  </div>

  <hr class="section-divider" />

  <section class="screener-section" id="screener">
    <div class="section-label">
      <div class="accent-bar" style="background:#10d98a"></div>
      <h2 style="color:#10d98a">Breakout Screener</h2>
      <span class="label-meta" id="screener-meta">· 4-filter gate: 4wk+ coil · vol ≥1.5× · RS&gt;SPY 3mo · &gt;50/200 SMA</span>
      <div class="label-action">
        <span class="lbl-time" id="screener-gen-time"></span>
        <button class="refresh-btn" style="color:#10d98a" id="screener-refresh-btn">⟳ Refresh</button>
      </div>
    </div>
    <div id="screener-regime"></div>

    <div class="scr-filters">
      <select class="scr-filter" id="scr-filter-sector"><option value="all">All sectors</option></select>
      <select class="scr-filter" id="scr-filter-exchange">
        <option value="all">All exchanges</option>
        <option value="NASDAQ">NASDAQ</option>
        <option value="NYSE">NYSE</option>
        <option value="AMEX">AMEX</option>
      </select>
      <select class="scr-filter" id="scr-gate" title="Relaxed: surface names on RS + trend (volume/base are dead factors, scored not gated). Strict: classic 4-filter breakout-from-base on a volume spike.">
        <option value="relaxed">Relaxed gate</option>
        <option value="strict">Strict breakout</option>
      </select>
      <select class="scr-filter" id="scr-mom" title="Momentum threshold: show only names whose trailing 3-month (63-session) return clears this bar. +11% was the validated large-cap top-quartile big-mover bar (Big-Mover Reveal, lift 1.41) — the one signal with statistical backing for catching big moves.">
        <option value="0">Momentum: any</option>
        <option value="11">3-mo ≥ +11% (top-quartile)</option>
        <option value="20">3-mo ≥ +20%</option>
        <option value="30">3-mo ≥ +30%</option>
        <option value="50">3-mo ≥ +50%</option>
      </select>
      <button class="scr-hc" id="scr-hc-toggle">🎯 High-Conviction</button>
      <button class="scr-hc" id="scr-emerg-toggle" title="Early movers only: names at the START of a momentum-emergence leg (fresh RS leadership + accumulation, not yet extended). Catches the RKLB-style early mover; cannot catch oversold-bounce/squeeze spikes like FCEL.">🌱 Emerging</button>
      <button class="scr-hc" id="scr-model-toggle" title="Rank by the learned model's win-probability">🤖 Model</button>
      <button class="scr-hc" id="scr-hidesi-toggle" title="Hide names with high short interest (SI ≥ 20% of shares or ≥ 7 days-to-cover). Research: high short interest is a significant NEGATIVE forward-return predictor (t=−3.76) — but short-side and regime-fragile (it inverted in the 2025 squeeze), so this is an OPT-IN soft avoidance filter, off by default.">🩳 Hide High SI</button>
    </div>
    <div class="scr-hc-def">
      <span id="scr-hc-deftext"></span>
      <label class="scr-hc-winlabel">edges from
        <select class="scr-hc-win" id="scr-hc-window">
          <option value="3">3-mo</option>
          <option value="6">6-mo</option>
          <option value="12">12-mo</option>
        </select>
      </label>
    </div>

    <details class="scr-help">
      <summary>📖 New here? How to use this screen &amp; its filters</summary>
      <div class="scr-help-body">
        <p><b>What this is.</b> A daily scan of the S&amp;P 500 (plus <b>small-cap</b> and <b>micro-cap</b> sections lower down) for stocks breaking out of a base on strength — potential moves <i>before</i> they get obvious. Each card shows a suggested <b>Entry</b>, <b>Stop</b> (where you're wrong), and <b>Target</b>.</p>
        <p class="scr-help-h">The filters — what each one does and when to use it:</p>
        <ul>
          <li><b>Sector</b> — narrow to one industry (e.g. only Technology). Use it to focus on a theme you like or avoid one you don't.</li>
          <li><b>Exchange</b> — filter by listing venue (NASDAQ/NYSE/AMEX). Most people can leave this on "All."</li>
          <li><b>Relaxed gate vs Strict breakout</b> — how picky the scan is. <b>Relaxed</b> (default) shows strong trending names broadly. <b>Strict</b> shows only textbook breakouts (tight base + volume spike) — far fewer names, higher bar. Start Relaxed; switch to Strict when you want only the cleanest setups.</li>
          <li><b>Momentum (3-mo ≥ X%)</b> — only show names already up at least X% over the last 3 months. <b>+11% = "top-quartile"</b>, the level our own study found best concentrates big movers. Use it to skip laggards and keep only names with real momentum behind them.</li>
          <li><b>🎯 High-Conviction</b> — only setups that match the edges our backtest actually proved out-of-sample. Use it when you want quality over quantity.</li>
          <li><b>🌱 Emerging</b> — early movers just <i>starting</i> a leg up (fresh leadership + accumulation, not yet stretched). Use it to get in early, RKLB-style. <i>Note: it can't catch oversold-bounce/squeeze spikes like FCEL — those launch from weakness and aren't predictable here.</i></li>
          <li><b>🤖 Model</b> — re-orders the list by a learned win-probability model instead of the default score. Use it to see which names the model likes most.</li>
        </ul>
        <p class="scr-help-h">A simple way to start:</p>
        <ol>
          <li>Leave it on <b>Relaxed</b>, optionally set <b>Momentum ≥ +11%</b> and turn on <b>🎯 High-Conviction</b> to get a short, strong list.</li>
          <li>Open a card and read its <b>Entry / Stop / Target</b> — and confirm on a real chart (TradingView) before doing anything.</li>
          <li><b>Size by risk:</b> shares ≈ (1% of your account) ÷ (Entry − Stop).</li>
          <li>Watch the <b>⚠ liquidity badge</b> — thin names can be hard to get in and out of.</li>
        </ol>
        <p class="scr-help-note">This is research to help you find candidates — not financial advice. Always do your own check before buying.</p>
      </div>
    </details>

    <div class="scr-tune">
      <button class="scr-tune-toggle" id="scr-tune-toggle"><span>⚙ Tune Quant Weights</span><span class="ct-arrow">▾</span></button>
      <div class="scr-tune-body" id="scr-tune-body" style="display:none">
        <div class="scr-tune-seg" id="scr-tune-tiers"></div>
        <div class="scr-tune-presets" id="scr-tune-presets"></div>
        <div id="scr-tune-sliders"></div>
        <div class="scr-tune-actions">
          <button class="refresh-btn" id="scr-tune-reset">↺ Reset tier</button>
          <button class="refresh-btn" id="scr-tune-all">⇊ Apply to all tiers</button>
          <button class="refresh-btn" id="scr-tune-export">⤴ Export</button>
          <button class="refresh-btn" id="scr-tune-import">⤵ Import</button>
          <span class="scr-tune-note">Per-tier · saved on this device</span>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>

    <div class="scr-rankby" id="rankby-large"></div>
    <div id="screener-container">
      <div class="mom-status"><div class="mom-spinner"></div><p>Scanning the S&amp;P 500 for accumulation breakouts…</p></div>
    </div>

    <div class="scr-subhead">🔬 Small-Cap Breakouts <span class="sub-meta" id="screener-small-meta"></span></div>
    <div class="scr-rankby" id="rankby-small"></div>
    <div id="screener-small-container">

        <button class="bt-view-btn active" id="bt-view-trades" data-view="trades">Trade Sim</button>
        <button class="bt-view-btn" id="bt-view-wf" data-view="walkforward">Walk-Forward (GAI)</button>
        <button class="bt-view-btn" id="bt-view-movers" data-view="movers">Big-Mover Reveal</button>

      </div>

    <div id="sector-heatmap-container">
      <div class="sector-grid" style="opacity:0.3">
        ${['SPY','QQQ','XLK','XLF','XLV','XLE','XLI','XLY','XLP','XLB','XLRE','XLU'].map(s=>`<div class="sector-tile" style="border-color:var(--border);background:var(--card)"><div class="st-sym" style="color:var(--text-dim)">${s}</div><div class="st-pct" style="color:var(--text-muted)">—</div></div>`).join('')}
      </div>
    </div>
  </section>

  <!-- 🕵️ SHARP MONEY — informed-activity hallmarks in prediction markets -->
  <section class="momentum-section" id="sharp">
    <div class="section-label">
      <div class="accent-bar" style="background:#e11d48"></div>
      <h2 style="color:#e11d48">🕵️ Sharp Money</h2>
      <span class="label-meta">· hallmarks of potentially-informed betting (size + conviction)</span>
      <div class="label-action">
        <span class="lbl-time" id="sharp-gen-time"></span>
        <button class="refresh-btn" style="color:#e11d48" id="sharp-refresh-btn">⟳ Refresh</button>
      </div>
    </div>
``````
